## reference · human-AI interaction guidelines
* There are 18 Guidelines for Human-AI Interaction.
* The Guidelines are evidence-based best practices for designing AI user experiences.
* The Guidelines can be found in the HAX Design Library.
* The Guidelines cover four phases of interaction: initial interaction, during interaction, when the AI system is wrong, and over time.
* The Guidelines were developed based on over 20 years of research, introduced in a 2019 CHI paper.
* The Guidelines are a synthesis of research and were validated through a process described on the About page of the HAX Toolkit.
* The HAX Workbook can be used to prioritize which Guidelines to apply and how to implement them.
* Design patterns for implementing the Guidelines can be found in the HAX Design Library.
Sources: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/haxtoolkit/ai-guidelines/
